description: Length-matched V2 facts for proof techniques, barriers, and methods for attacking unsolved problems.

facts.context: Diagonalization arguments (Cantor, Turing) prove impossibility results by:
facts.truth: constructing objects that differ from every element in a countable list at that element's index
distractors: enumerating all elements in the list and checking each one satisfies a fixed algebraic constraint
applying matrix eigenvalue decomposition to transform the list into an orthogonal basis set
reducing the problem to a finite combinatorial search over bounded-length binary strings

facts.context: Relativization barrier for P vs NP shows that:
facts.truth: there exist oracles A and B where P^A = NP^A and P^B ≠ NP^B
distractors: for every oracle C the classes P^C and NP^C coincide, making the question trivial
oracle separations definitively resolve P vs NP because oracles model all computation
there exists a single universal oracle that simultaneously decides every complexity question

facts.context: The algebrization barrier (Aaronson-Wigderson 2009) extends relativization showing:
facts.truth: techniques that algebrize cannot separate P from NP
distractors: algebraic extensions of oracles always collapse P and NP into a single class
algebrization is strictly weaker than relativization and blocks fewer proof strategies
algebrizing techniques have already resolved the P vs NP question for bounded-depth circuits

facts.context: Geometric Complexity Theory (GCT) approaches P vs NP using:
facts.truth: algebraic geometry and representation theory to prove circuit lower bounds
distractors: purely combinatorial Ramsey-type arguments applied to Boolean function truth tables
topological fixed-point theorems that characterize the structure of feasible computations
probabilistic polynomial identity testing to distinguish easy from hard function families

facts.context: Cohen's forcing technique in set theory was used to prove:
facts.truth: independence of Continuum Hypothesis from ZFC (neither provable nor refutable)
distractors: that the Continuum Hypothesis is true in every transitive model of Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory
that the Continuum Hypothesis is false and the continuum has cardinality exactly aleph-two
that forcing is unable to address questions about the cardinality of the real number continuum

facts.context: The compactness theorem in model theory states:
facts.truth: a theory has a model iff every finite subset has a model
distractors: every consistent first-order theory has a unique model up to elementary equivalence
compactness applies only to theories with a finite number of non-logical symbols
compactness fails for standard first-order logic and holds only for infinitary languages

facts.context: The Hardy-Littlewood circle method is used in additive number theory by:
facts.truth: analyzing generating functions via complex contour integrals, separating major and minor arcs
distractors: directly counting integer solutions using elementary combinatorial inclusion-exclusion sieving
constructing geometric circles in the complex plane and measuring their intersection areas
applying the method exclusively to estimate the distribution of prime numbers below a bound

facts.context: Modern sieve methods in number theory provide:
facts.truth: upper bounds on primes in sets but cannot prove infinitude (parity barrier)
distractors: exact counts of prime numbers in arbitrary arithmetic progressions without any error term
direct lower bounds on prime density that immediately resolve the twin prime conjecture
a complete unconditional proof that there are infinitely many twin primes in every residue class

facts.context: Ricci flow, used by Perelman to prove Poincaré, evolves metric by:
facts.truth: ∂g/∂t = -2Ric(g), deforming geometry to find canonical structures
distractors: ∂g/∂t = +2Ric(g), uniformly increasing curvature everywhere until the manifold collapses
restricting the evolution to two-dimensional surfaces where singularities cannot form at all
holding volume constant at every time step so the total geometric size never changes

facts.context: Modular forms were essential to Wiles' proof of Fermat's Last Theorem via:
facts.truth: the modularity theorem linking elliptic curves to modular forms
distractors: direct algebraic manipulation of the Fermat equation using Galois group actions alone
exhaustive computer enumeration of solutions up to a provably sufficient numerical bound
a partial result covering only regular primes, leaving the irregular case still unresolved

facts.context: Paris-Harrington theorem shows that certain combinatorial statements:
facts.truth: are true but unprovable in Peano arithmetic (first natural independence result)
distractors: are provable within Peano arithmetic using standard induction on the natural numbers
are false in the standard model of arithmetic despite being consistent with the axioms
require the full axiom system of ZFC set theory even to be formally stated as sentences

facts.context: Reverse mathematics classifies theorems by the axioms needed to prove them. Most analysis theorems need:
facts.truth: WKL₀ or ACA₀ (weak systems much weaker than full ZFC)
distractors: the full power of ZFC together with large cardinal axioms beyond measurable cardinals
only the basic axioms of Robinson arithmetic with no form of induction at all
no axioms whatsoever since analysis theorems are all logically valid tautologies
